rulings.Case Title: MARK BECHELLI ET AL VS. JOHN C. KILROY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants' Notice Of Motion And Motion To Bifurcate Trial Proceedings
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 1, 2023 line 1. DEFENDANTS' NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO BIFURCATE TRIAL PROCEEDINGS is DENIED without prejudice to be decided by the trial judge. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: MARK BECHELLI ET AL VS. JOHN C. KILROY ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 1, 2023 line 1. DEFENDANTS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Neither party requested permission to file oversized briefs despite this motion being continued twice. Therefore, excessive briefs filed by both parties are stricken although considered. The motion is granted as to the First cause of action for Breach of Contract based on lack of consideration. The benefit Plaintiff-promisee conferred on Defendant-promisor of not challenging Defendant's entitlements was not bargained for as the exchange for the promise to maintain level rent. (Property California SCJLW One Corp. v. Leamy (2018) 25 Cal.App.5th 1155, 1165; Def.'s UMF, No. 7; Dennis Decl., Exh. W, p. 226:24-228:20.) The motion is denied on all other grounds. The motion is denied as to the Second Cause of Action for Fraud and Deceit. There is a triable issue of fact whether Plaintiffs relied on the October 2014 Promise, and whether that reliance was reasonable. Plaintiff sufficiently alleges the harm and the Economic Loss Rule does not bar any of Plaintiff's claims. (Erlich v. Menezes (1999) 21 Cal.4th 543, 553-554; see also Harris v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (1993) 14 Cal.App.4th 70, 78.) The motion is denied as to the Third Cause of Action for Promissory Estoppel. There is a triable issue of fact whether there was a clear promise, whether Plaintiffs relied on that promise, and whether reliance was reasonable. The motion is granted as to the Fourth Cause of Action for Breach of Warranty of Authority. This claim requires the existence of a contract. (See Jeppi v. Brockman Holding Co. (1949) 34 Cal.2d 11, 18-19; CC Section 2343.) The motion is moot as to the declaratory relief. Plaintiffs are no longer in possession of the Premises and will dismiss their claim. The motion for summary adjudication as to the claims against John Kilroy in his individual capacity is denied. There is a triable issue of fact of whether Mr. Kilroy participated, authorized, or directed the alleged fraud. Plaintiff's claims are not barred by Res Judicata. Claim preclusion arises if a second suit involves: (1) the same cause of action (2) between the same parties (3) after a final judgment on the merits in the first suit. (Mycogen Corp. v. Monsanto Co. (2002) 28 Cal.4th 888, 896.) Defendant fails to establish prong 3. The arbitrator only had authority to determine the Fair Market Rent of the lease option. (Plfs. Evidence Ex. 1 at 2 Ex. 1.1.) =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: JALASAI BAPU, LLC VS. ARTHUR MARBELLA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendant Arthur Marbella To Respond To Written Discovery, Produce Documents, And To Deem Matters Admitted
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 1, 2023 line 6. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL DEFENDANT ARTHUR MARBELLA TO RESPOND TO WRITTEN DISCOVERY, PRODUCE DOCUMENTS, AND TO DEEP MATTERS ADMITTED is GRANTED, subject to opposition. Defendant has 5 days to comply from service of the entry of the order. Defendant Marbella is to provide substantive responses, without objections, to: (1) Requests for Production of Documents to Defendant Arthur Marbella, Set One; (2) Special Interrogatories to Defendant Arthur Marbella, Set One; (3) Form Interrogatories - General to Defendant Arthur Marbella, Set One; and (4) Form Interrogatories - Unlawful Detainer to Defendant Arthur Marbella, Set One. All matters from Request for Admissions to Defendant Arthur Marbella, Set One are deemed admitted. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
